title: What the War Against Iran Means for the U.S.-South Korean Alliance
summary:

That mission remains indispensable, but a serious disruption in the Strait of Hormuz now hits South Korea directly through energy imports, shipping, industrial production, and economic confidence. title: Iran war live: Trump urges Tehran to ‘give up’; Ghalibaf mocks US strategy
summary: Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf ridicules US's economic pressure campaign as oil prices surge and Hormuz standoff continues.

title: Chile police blast water cannons at student protesters

title: Carney ‘strong’ in year one, now must deliver on promises in Canada
summary: Canada's prime minister has used Trump's attacks on allies and others to refocus Ottawa's foreign policy.

title: Israel has begun intercepting Gaza-bound Global Sumud Flotilla aid boats
summary: Organisers of a Gaza-bound aid mission say their vessels are being intercepted by Israeli military speedboats.

title: Israeli forces raid Global Sumud Flotilla boats in international waters
summary: Seven out of 58 vessels were captured by Israel near Crete, as flotilla aims to deliver aid to Gaza under blockade.

title: UAE quits OPEC
summary: The UAE announced it’s withdrawing from OPEC on May 1st.

title: Arsenal cruelly denied penalty by VAR in Champions League draw at Atletico
summary: Arsenal scored one penalty, but VAR controversially denied them another in a 1-1 semifinal draw at Atletico Madrid.

title: New Florida map boosts Republican seats amid national redistricting fight
summary: Florida has become latest state to redraw map, as national electoral landscape transforms before midterm elections.

title: Trump: Putin offered to help settle Iranian nuclear enrichment impasse
summary: Donald Trump said that Vladimir Putin told him he’d “like to be involved” in settling Iranian nuclear enrichment.

title: Why is the UAE quitting OPEC – and what’s the impact?
summary: The decision by the United Arab Emirates comes during the worst global energy crisis of modern times.

title: What’s behind Israel’s plan to move the Bnei Menashe?
summary: The new wave of the Bnei Menashe community from Northeast India have arrived in Israel under a 2026 government plan.

title: Ukraine asks Israel to seize ship carrying grain ‘stolen’ by Russia
summary: Kyiv requested that Israel seize the vessel and its cargo, take grain ‌samples, and question crew ​members.

title: Musk accuses Altman of betraying OpenAI’s nonprofit founding mission
summary: OpenAI’s lawyers argue Musk’s lawsuit aims to undermine the company for his competing venture, xAI.

title: Mali’s Tuareg rebels say Russian fighters must withdraw from country
summary: As military government battles to reassert control, spokesperson for Tuareg rebels says it will fall 'sooner or later'.

title: Ex-FBI chief Comey appears in court over alleged Trump threat
summary: Comey turned himself in on two charges, threatening the US president's life and transmitting threats across state lines.

title: US lawmakers question Pete Hegseth over budget for war on Iran
summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth testified before Congress on the Pentagon’s budget for the US-Israeli war on Iran.

title: Pentagon chief Hegseth first public hearing on Iran war: Key takeaways
summary: Pete Hegseth testifies alongside General Dan Caine, responds to questions on war Pentagon says has cost $25bn.

title: Two Jewish men stabbed in London ‘terrorist attack’
summary: Two Jewish men were stabbed in north London, after a man armed with a knife was seen running through the area.

title: Indonesian soldiers on trial after acid attack on activist
summary: Four Indonesian soldiers are on trial for an acid attack on a prominent activist in Jakarta.

title: ‘Trump’s Justice Department is on a revenge tour’
summary: Claire Finkelstein says Comey will likely fight his indictment over a social media post critical of Trump.

title: How AI-led deflation is dragging Indian IT firms but spurring innovation
summary: AI-driven deflation is becoming a reality, with a base-case estimate of 3.5% for the industry, even as a timing lag in new programme ramp-ups is leading to weaker-than-expected growth guidance. While stock valuations appear inexpensive, the sector currently lacks strong near-term triggers, with mid-tier players seen as better positioned than incumbents.

title: Castrol India plans price hikes, cost cuts as crude, FX volatility persists: CFO Srinivasan
summary: Castrol India is implementing a multi-pronged strategy including price hikes, cost interventions, and enhanced supplier negotiations to counter currency and crude oil price volatility. The company is prepared for further price adjustments and potential supply chain disruptions, while maintaining a strong focus on rural market expansion and exploring new segments like data centers.

title: India’s consulting sector at inflection point; firms must scale up as growth accelerates: Practus CEO
summary: India’s consulting sector is at a turning point as growth accelerates, but can domestic firms scale up, close technology gaps and build proprietary IP to compete? Deepak Narayanan says demand will surge, but structural challenges must be addressed to capture the opportunity.

title: Bata India plans to reach 3,000 stores in 3-4 yrs, targets tier three and tier four cities: CFO Amit Aggarwal
summary: Bata India is expanding its reach into smaller cities using a franchise model. The company aims to grow its store count significantly in the coming years. Bata is focused on profitable growth and protecting its margins. Digital channels are also a key area for expansion. The company is exploring technology investments to improve operations.

title: How CFOs are facing currency volatility due to increased geopolitical tensions
summary: CFOs and treasurers are responding by revisiting hedging, pricing and funding decisions, with a growing emphasis on avoiding reactive moves driven by short-term market swings.

title: NFRA flags absence of PPAs, inadequate ECL documentation and independence declaration gaps at B D G & CO LLP
summary: Audit regulator cites missing revenue audit records, lack of loan recoverability documentation and SQC misalignment in 2024 inspection.

title: Exclusive: Govt mulls CAG-like body for listed firms, private banks, NBFCs
summary: While public sector undertakings already come under the CAG led framework, the proposal currently being examined by a high-level PMO panel seeks to extend a similar model first to publicly listed companies, private sector banks and NBFCs, where auditor appointments are currently driven by boards and shareholders.

title: AB Energia targets to sustain 2.5x growth, plans backward integration, BESS, & scale to ₹1,000 cr revenue: CFO Sandesh Naik
summary: AB Energia, a solar EPC firm, aims to maintain its 2.5x growth trajectory by expanding its core business and diversifying into new verticals like battery storage and residential rooftop solar. The company projects Rs 1,000 crore revenue in FY27, driven by both its associate IPP and external clients, while also exploring international markets.

title: NFRA flags insufficient audit evidence and incorrect opinion in Singhi & Co audit, finds gaps in independence and documentation
summary: FY24 inspection highlights unsupported audit opinion, weak file integrity, inconsistent sampling and control testing.

title: India Inc net profit rising in double digits in Q4 despite RIL's dip
summary: Corporate earnings for the March 2026 quarter show strong growth. Aggregate net profit rose double digits, boosted by banking and finance firms. Reliance Industries' profit declined, but this was offset by other sectors. Revenue growth reached its highest in seven quarters. IT companies also reported significant profit increases. More results are expected soon, offering further insights.

title: Trump's $166 billion tariff refunds may finally be in sight as first payouts expected by May 11
summary: The Trump administration is set to issue its first tariff refunds around May 11, following the Supreme Court's ruling that the sweeping duties were illegal. A new process, CAPE, is handling the refunds, with approximately 3% of affected entries already in the payment stage. This process could cover billions in duties paid by hundreds of thousands of importers.

title: Govt reviews divestment plans, IDBI Bank stake sale
summary: Govt officials are reviewing the IDBI Bank disinvestment strategy. This comes after weak investor interest and market volatility impacted the proposed sale. A core group of secretaries is deliberating on revised valuations and timelines. Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman confirmed the strategic sale will proceed. The government aims to conclude the IDBI Bank transaction soon.

title: 70% of Indian goods to enter New Zealand duty-free under FTA; big boost for MSMEs, says Piyush Goyal
summary: Union Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal on Sunday said India and New Zealand will sign a long-pending bilateral trade agreement on Monday, marking a key step to boost trade ties between the two countries. Speaking during his visit to Agra with New Zealand's Minister for Trade and Investment, Todd McClay, Goyal said the agreement was finalised after years of discussions at the highest level.

title: MPC minutes signal extended RBI pause as West Asia conflict clouds outlook
summary: The minutes of the April policy meeting, where the MPC unanimously held the repo rate at 5.25% and retained a neutral stance, indicate that policymakers are viewing the current inflationary pressures as supply-driven and are willing to look through first-round effects while preserving flexibility for future action

title: Nirmala Sitharaman, Piyush Goyal hold talks with Korean leaders on future bilateral cooperation
summary: India and South Korea are strengthening economic ties. Union ministers met with South Korean leaders and business executives. Discussions focused on financial sectors, startups, and trade expansion. Both nations aim to increase bilateral trade to 50 billion dollars by 2030. This collaboration will drive investment and innovation between the two countries.

title: Iran-US war: Strait of Hormuz disruptions have implications for India, says Rajnath Singh
summary: In Germany, he urged stronger India–Germany defence ties, expanded industrial cooperation, and coordinated responses to emerging security challenges including cyber, AI and drone warfare.

title: First tranche of India-US trade deal almost finalised: Piyush Goyal
summary: Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal said on Monday that the first tranche of the India-US bilateral trade agreement has almost been finalised, and a team of his ministry officials is in Washington for the discussions.

title: RBI plans to remain nimble to prevent persistent supply shock amid West Asia crisis
summary: Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra addressed global challenges at Princeton University, stressing upon agile monetary policy to counter supply shocks from West Asia. The focus is on preventing inflation from becoming persistent. Prudent fiscal management and domestic production also play key roles, he said.

title: Gig workers social security scheme may have multiple fund managers
summary: One possibility under consideration is roping in the Employees' Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) under the ministry to handle provident fund contribution and disbursement, while outsourcing pension to another fund manager.

title: Delhi to put its chips in semiconductor gambit with new policy push
summary: According to govt officials, the policy will be built around five key pillars: semiconductor design and intellectual property development, research and innovation, manufacturing-enabling segments such as assembly, testing, marking and packaging (ATMP) and OSAT, talent development, and a strengthened startup ecosystem.

title: Trump and a camel's dream: Where exactly are US-Iran talks headed?
summary: US President Trump claims a deal with Iran is imminent, with Tehran agreeing to surrender enriched uranium. However, Iran has sarcastically dismissed these assertions. Despite diverging narratives, Pakistan facilitates ongoing talks for a second negotiation round, aiming to de-escalate regional tensions and potentially secure an interim understanding.

title: Textiles Ministry plans duty cuts to shield industry amid West Asia crisis
summary: In a bid to navigate the turbulent waters of global shipping issues, India's textiles ministry is advocating for lower duties on rayon pulp and certain cotton varieties. Furthermore, there's potential for a postponement of anti-dumping duties on specific yarns, which would significantly aid supply chains.

title: India-UK free trade pact may come into force from second week of May: Official
summary: "We are expecting the pact to be implemented from the second week of May," the official said.

title: West Asia War: Trump says 50% tariff threat on aiding Iranian military applies to China as well as peace talks stall
summary: Trump had earlier said any country providing military equipment to Iran would be hit with tariffs of up to 50% on all exports to the United States.

title: RBI's three major decisions to boost Ease of Doing Business: Explained
summary: The Reserve Bank of India on Wednesday introduces a set of measures aimed at enhancing Ease of Doing Business, including easing board level compliance requirements, consolidating supervisory instructions, and removing due diligence norms for MSMEs on trade platforms.

title: RBI MPC holds repo rate at 5.25%, maintains neutral stance
summary: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee has maintained the key policy repo rate at 5.25 percent, holding its neutral stance. This decision balances inflation concerns with domestic growth, supported by steady demand and fiscal measures. This marks the fourth consecutive pause in the current interest rate cycle.

title: RBI likely to maintain 'status quo' on repo rates, address key macro issues: Economists
summary: The RBI is set to announce the outcome of its first MPC meeting for FY 26-27 on Wednesday, April 8. The current repo rate stands at 5.25 per cent. It was last changed in December 2025, when the central bank had cut the repo rate by 25 basis points to 5.25 per cent.

title: Government issues draft norms to standardise state, district GDP estimates
summary: The key focus areas in SDP include identification and integration of new data sources such as administrative records, sector-specific databases and survey inputs to improve coverage and reliability, and refinement of estimation techniques to better capture emerging sectors, particularly services and the unincorporated segment.

title: RBI MPC Meeting: India seen holding rates as 'Goldilocks' phase gives way to stress
summary: Instead, the focus is likely to be on calming financial markets, with assurances of readiness to support the weakening rupee and inject liquidity to keep bond yields in check, economists and traders said.

title: RBI may keep rates unchanged, focus on rupee stability and bond yields
summary: The six-member monetary policy committee meets April 6-8 for the first time since the war broke out on February 28.
